_comment: Cross-archive concordance overlay. Keyed by record permalink key (caV-dN-nSCAN). Maintained by hand from vault projects/ca-archives/ca-cross-archive-concordance.md. kind: original|copy|counterpart|related|published. SJ = San José Pueblo Papers, History San José (Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ypmXyghhLi0wiL9HjNJ8kQAx7ZtRiHbx; scans keyed by the HSJ Current Index CSV).

ca12-d465-n391.text: The Mexico-City end of this affair survives as an original: Boris Tarasov's asylum petition, AGN (Mexico City), Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 — enclosing Gov. Solá's 10 Nov 1815 dispatch. The Californian and viceregal sides of the same capture. Cf. Mathes, La frontera ruso-mexicana (1990).

ca12-d466-n391.text: The Mexico-City end of this affair survives as an original: Boris Tarasov's asylum petition, AGN (Mexico City), Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57. Cf. Mathes (1990).

ca13-d273-n216.text: Counterpart original in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov's asylum petition and the bureaucratic file San Blas→Mexico→Cádiz). This Savage copy is the sole survivor of the California-side inventory; the originals burned in 1906.

ca13-d274-n217.text: Counterpart original in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum file).

ca17-d173-n114.text: The captured hunter of this report, Boris Tarasov, generated a counterpart file in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (his asylum petition, ending 'se fugó para Panamá'). Cf. Mathes (1990).

ca17-d174-n115.text: Counterpart file in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum petition).

ca17-d192-n153.text: The interrogated prisoner's Mexico-City file survives: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum petition; encloses Solá's 10 Nov 1815 dispatch).

ca14-d160-n177.text: The pueblo's received original survives: San José Pueblo Papers (History San José), scan 1979-861-0021 — Arrillaga to Cabo Macario Castro, 29 Dec 1793, 'Sobre repartición de tierras,' the settler land-distribution rules (English translation by Pinoris on the HSJ Drive). Pairing verified against this Savage abstract (leaf n178), 2026-07-05.

ca12-d102-n108.text: The pueblo's copies survive, signed and with the cover letter: San José Pueblo Papers scans 1979-861-0457a & 0457b — two signed copies (Arrillaga and Pablo Vicente de Solá) of the same 13-regulation set for the San José militia-artillery company, dated 10 Feb 1806 — and scan 1979-861-0456, Arrillaga's 12 Jul 1806 transmittal to Comisionado Macario Castro enclosing the instructions. The C-A copy (this record) is the unsigned 12 Jul text ('Instrucciones para el servicio de la Comp.ª miliciana de Artill.ª,' sin firma). Verified on leaf n108, 2026-07-05.

ca18-d100-n138.text: The inventories behind this handover survive: San José Pueblo Papers scans 1979-861-0649 (chapel ornaments & sacred vessels) and 1979-861-0650 (justice-of-the-peace office, incl. the pueblo archive vols. 1–38, 1785–1822), both 22 Oct 1822, signed Peralta and Robles — 'entrega del archivo y existencias… á Juan Alvires, Alcalde del Ayuntam.te.' Verified on leaf n138, 2026-07-05.

ca19-d68-n63.text: The San José copy of this notice survives: San José Pueblo Papers scan 1979-861-0967 — Zamorano's 6 Sep 1830 aviso on land and cattle titles, the very 'aviso igual… á los de San José Guadalupe' this abstract records. Verified on leaf n63, 2026-07-05.

ca12-d237-n214.text: The comisionado's copy of the punishment order survives: San José Pueblo Papers scan 1979-861-0488 — Arrillaga to the San José comisionado, 17 Aug 1807: the same order this abstract routes through Estudillo ('le manda al Comisionado haga traer al pueblo al indio cristiano de Sta Clara…'). Verified on leaf n214, 2026-07-05.

ca12-d301-n255.text: The San José end of this dispute survives: San José Pueblo Papers scan 1979-861-0546 — Estudillo to the comisionado, 12 Nov 1809, ordering Father Durán's livestock off the contested lands, two days before this reply of Arrillaga on the pueblo–mission límites. Verified on leaf n255, 2026-07-05.

ca8-d360-n358.text: The instruction behind this report survives: San José Pueblo Papers scan 1979-861-0136 — Sal to Moraga, 2 May 1797, the firearms-distribution and registration instructions whose lists ('la adjunta lista… sus distribuciones') Moraga forwards here to Borica. Verified on leaf n358, 2026-07-05.

ca14-d1122-n272.text: The San José end of the same invasion-contingency package survives: San José Pueblo Papers scan 1979-861-0166 — Alberni to the comisionado, 22 Oct 1797, transmitting Borica's decree that if the governor were captured, authorities must resist and ignore orders extracted from him; two days before these instructions to Córdoba in the same English-invasion scare. C-A side verified on leaf n272, 2026-07-05.

ca6-d192-n276.text: The viceregal end of the Bodega-occupation operation survives: AGN (Mexico City), Californias 9, cuaderno 19 — the Revillagigedo ↔ Bodega y Quadra occupation orders and schooner instructions of Feb–Mar 1793 (per Inglis 2012). This 6 Jul 1793 order to the governor is the provincial arm of the same operation. C-A side scan-verified 2026-07-02.

ca55-d335-n297.text: Matute's own report survives at Mexico City: AGN, Historia vol. 70 — Juan Bautista Matute on the naval attempt at Bodega (Jul–Oct 1793), published in English by Wagner, California Historical Society Quarterly X:4 (1931), 339–341. The naval-chain twin of the report Sal forwards here. C-A side scan-verified 2026-07-02.
